person.name: Agnes de Roos

memoryArtifacts.headline: Arms of de Ros: Gules, three water bougets argent.

burialPlace.address: Kirkham, Ryedale District, North Yorkshire, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Agnes de Roos was the daughter of William de Roos, Knt., 1st Lord Roos of Helmsley and Maud de Vaux, daughter and coheir of John de Vaux, Knt. Her birthdate is estimated to be about 1295.

Agnes married first, before 3 September 1311, to Pain de Tibetot (or Tybotot), Knt., 1st Baron Tiptoft, second but surviving son of Robert de Tiptoft, Knt., and Eve de Chaworth. 

«b»They had one son.«/b»
1.) John, Knt., 2nd Lord Tibetot, was born 20 July 1313 and died 6 (or 13) April 1367. He married first to Margaret de Badlesmere, and second to Elizabeth Aspall.

Sir Pain died testate, having been killed in the Battle of Bannockburn on 24 June 1314. 

Agnes married second, before 17 June 1315 without license to Thomas de Vere, Knt. (she was his second wife). They had no children.

Agnes died shortly before 25 November 1328 and Thomas died shortly before 12 May 1329.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de Roos於大約1285年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，她的父親Sir William de Ros II32歲，母親Maud de Vaux26歲。她於1311年9月3日之前與Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1328年11月25日在英國英格蘭素福爾可內特萊斯亞德享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾吉可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Roos
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch (also De Roos): topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a rose, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who grew roses, from roos ‘rose’.
Dutch: from the female personal name Rosa (from Latin rosa ‘rose’).
Dutch: nickname from roos ‘erysipelas’, an infection which causes reddening of the skin and scalp, applied presumably to someone with a ruddy complexion.

variants.name: Roose
meanings: German: variant of   Rose   and   Roos  .

variants.name: Roosen
meanings: Dutch and Flemish: patronymic from   Roos   1–3.

variants.name: Rose
meanings: English, Scottish, French, Walloon, Danish, and German: from the name of the flower, Middle English, Old French, Middle High German rose (from Latin rosa), in various applications. In part, it is a topographic name for someone who lived at a place where wild roses grew, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house bearing the sign of the rose. It is also found, especially in Europe, as a nickname for a man with a ‘rosy’ complexion (compare 4 below). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates and similar-sounding names from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Rózsa (see Rozsa ), Slovak Róža and Czech Roza . Compare 6 below and French Larose 2.
English: from the Middle English female personal name Rohese, Roese, later Rose, Royse (ancient Germanic Hrodohaidis, Rothaid, composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + haid(is) ‘kind, sort’).
English and Scottish: variant of   Ross  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de Roos was born about 1285, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Sir William de Ros II, was 32 and her mother, Maud de Vaux, was 26. She married Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot before 3 September 1311, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 25 November 1328, in Nettlestead, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44, and was buried in Kirkham, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de Roos est née environ 1285, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Ros II, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Maud de Vaux, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot avant 3 septembre 1311, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 25 novembre 1328, à Nettlestead, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Kirkham, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

September 1311 in England, United Kingdom Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb am 25. it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Roos è nata il circa 1285 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Sir William de Ros II, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Maud de Vaux, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot prima del 3 settembre 1311, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 25 novembre 1328, nel Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Kirkham, North Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes de Roosが約1285年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父Sir William de Ros IIは32歳，母Maud de Vauxは26歳であった。1311年9月3日以前にイギリスイギリスでSir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetotと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1328年11月25日にイギリスイギリススフォルクネットレステアドで44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアキルクハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de Roos(이)가 대략 1285년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Ros II(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Vaux(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1311년 9월 3일 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1328년 11월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 네틀레스테아드에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 커카에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de Roos nasceu aproximadamente 1285, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Ros II, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Vaux, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot antes 3 de setembro de 1311, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 25 de novembro de 1328, em Nettlestead, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Kirkham, North Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de Roos родилась около 1285 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Ros II было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Maud de Vaux, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Payne de Tibetot 1st Baron Tybetot перед 3 сентябрь 1311 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла 25 ноября 1328 г. в местности неттлестид, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности кирхам, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
